revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
[[ja:粟皇子神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Awamiko Shrine (粟皇子神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Awamiko-jinja 01.jpg | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, Futami-cho, Matsushita, Toritori 1687-2 | Latitude degree = 34 | Latitude minute = 29 | Latitude second = 47.4 | Longitude degree = 136 | Longitude minute = 48 | Longitude second = 55.4 | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Susanoo-no-Mikoto Mitama-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine | Foundation = Before the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97379316}} (927 CE) | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | Alternate name = | Pilgrimage site = | festival = | Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} | location = {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.4965|N|136.815389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Awamiko Shrine''' (粟皇子神社) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of the {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, specifically of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingu|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine). It Ranks 19th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Uji-Yamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 Situated on a cape overlooking the scenic area of {{ill|Ikenoura|ja|池の浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikenoura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine enshrines a protective deity of the coastline.Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 98-99 == Overview == Awamiko Shrine is located at Toritori 1687-2, Matsushita, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 76 It is near Ikenoura Beach and is the shrine closest to the coastline among the 125 shrines that comprise the Ise Grand Shrine complex.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 71 The shrine grounds span approximately 419.8 square meters.Uji-Yamada City Office (1929): p. 11 The enshrined deity is Susanoo-no-Mikoto Mitama-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto (須佐乃乎命御玉道主命),Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 99 a goddess born from the sacred covenant (ukei) between {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐乃乎命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She is considered a guardian deity of the coastline. The goddess is also known as Awa no Miko no Kami (淡海子神). While traditionally associated with the {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} as described in the Japanese chronicles ({{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}), there are varying interpretations. == History == As one of the auxiliary shrines of the Ise Grand Shrine, Awamiko Shrine was established before the Completion of the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} in the 5th year of the {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97379316}} (927 CE).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 According to the {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, the shrine was named and consecration_year by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} during her journey through {{ill|Shima Province|en|Shima Province|qid=Q1053722|lt=Shima Province|1=Shima Province}}. Initially located on Nakajima Island off Ikenoura, the shrine’s worship practices lapsed over time. It was restored in 1692 (Genroku 5) and moved to its current location on Toritori Beach. Later, in 1712 (Shōtoku 2), it was relocated further inland due to damage from high tides. The current shrine building was reconstructed in 1908 (Meiji 41). == Access == Awamiko Shrine is located within the premises of the hotel "Ryoshō Umi no Chō." Visitors can request permission at the hotel front desk to access the shrine, with signage provided to guide the way.{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05: Futami Route - Shrines, Meotoiwa, and Somin Forest|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-03-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26|qq=}}{{Cite web|author=PV Liaison Committee|date=2012-08-21|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/volunteer/junshi/matsushita.pdf|title=Survey of Nature Trails in Ise-Shima National Park|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-03-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140315072829/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/volunteer/junshi/matsushita.pdf|archivedate=2014-03-15|qq=}} From {{ill|Matsushita Station|en|Matsushita Station|qid=Q93433|lt=Matsushita Station|1=Matsushita Station}} on the JR {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|qid=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}}, the shrine is approximately a 20-minute walk along a forest road leading to the beach.Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 96, 98 Visitors can also take a bus to Ikenoura Bus Stop, followed by a 40-minute walk. For drivers, the shrine is about 10 minutes (2.2 km) from Toba IC on the {{ill|Ise-Futami-Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Futami-Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378935}}, but access by car is limited to the hotel premises.{{Cite web|url=http://www.uminochou.com/spend/|title=Exploring Ryoshō Umi no Chō|publisher=Ryoshō Umi no Chō|accessdate=2014-03-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130813073726/http://www.uminochou.com/spend/|archivedate=2013-08-13|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Ise Bunka-sha|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka-sha|1=Ise Bunka-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11379080}}, ed. ''Oise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage,'' Special Issue of ''Isejin,'' Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Uji-Yamada City Office, ed. ''History of Uji-Yamada City (Volume 1),'' Uji-Yamada City Office, January 20, 1929. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q86741099}}, ed. ''Visiting Ise Shrine,'' Gakken Mook Shrine Pilgrimage Selection vol. 1, supervised by Minoru Sonoda, July 4, 2013. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * ''Ise Shrine and 125 Shrines Every Japanese Should Know,'' Tatsumi Publishing, November 1, 2013. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shrines in the Ise Grand Shrine Complex|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shrines in the Ise Grand Shrine Complex|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == *[http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1005.html Awamiko Shrine (粟皇子神社)] - Ise Grand Shrine Reverence Association {{125 Shrines of Ise|qq=}} {{external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{translated page|ja|粟皇子神社|version=98243921|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:粟皇子神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Awa-Miko Shrine (Q17192448)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|qid=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Awa-Miko Shrine|lt=Awamiko Shrine|ja|粟皇子神社|qid=Q17192448|1=Awa-Miko Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|qid=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || — || {{coord|34.496502|136.815394|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181135 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17192448}}==Notes== [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Futami Town]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]]
